compiled_state.title: ACLI subprocess calls have no timeout — a hung acli child freezes the whole reconcile pass
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Run any operation that invokes an ACLI subprocess (a reconcile pass: create/edit/transition/label/comment/link/delete).
2. Have the `acli` child stall — e.g. an interactive confirmation prompt, a stuck network connection at the acli layer, a Jira maintenance window, or a flag the installed acli mishandles (the `--json`-on-link-create/delete hang, fixed in bbad01da, was one instance).

Expected: the call times out and is retried/surfaced as an error within a bounded wall-clock budget, so a reconcile pass cannot hang indefinitely.
Actual: `acli_subprocess.py:206` calls `subprocess.run(full_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, env=env)` with NO `timeout=`. A non-returning acli child hangs the ENTIRE reconcile pass forever. The `_MAX_ATTEMPTS`/backoff retry logic only handles calls that RETURN an error — it does nothing for a call that never returns. (Observed empirically: a stalled acli call hung a process for 30+ minutes until manually cancelled.)

## Why this matters for clients
A real `rebar reconcile` issues many ACLI subprocess calls. The REST path (acli_rest.py `_rest_urlopen_with_retry`) is bounded (10-30s timeout, 3 attempts); the SUBPROCESS path is not. On a slow/rate-limited/prompting Jira, a client reconcile can hang with no watchdog. NOTE: this is NOT a same-store race — reconcile serializes same-machine passes via the advisory pass-lock; the gap is purely the unbounded subprocess call.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_run_acli` `subprocess.run` gets a bounded `timeout=` (e.g. configurable, default 60-120s)
- [ ] `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` is handled: kill/reap the child, treat as a retryable transient within `_MAX_ATTEMPTS` with backoff, and raise a clear error if exhausted
- [ ] Worst-case per-call (and thus per-pass) wall-clock is bounded; a hung acli child can never freeze a pass indefinitely
- [ ] Audit the other untimed subprocess.run calls (_advisory_lock.py, _attestation.py — git ops; lower risk but review)
- [ ] Unit test: a stubbed slow/hanging command surfaces TimeoutExpired and is bounded; suite green; ruff clean

comments.body:
CONFIRMED BY FALSIFIABLE EXPERIMENT (not just code reading).

Hypothesis: _run_acli (acli_subprocess.py:206), the chokepoint all reconcile ACLI subprocess calls funnel through (create/edit/transition/label/comment/link/delete via self._run), has no timeout, so a hung acli child blocks indefinitely.

Experiment: pointed _run_acli at a fake acli that blocks 30s (sh -c 'sleep 30'), observed for 10s in a thread.
- CONTROL: subprocess.run(same cmd, timeout=3) raised TimeoutExpired at 3.0s -> the measurement method DOES detect a bound when one exists.
- TEST: _run_acli STILL BLOCKING at 10s on the 30s-hanging child, no exception -> NO internal timeout. H1 CONFIRMED.

Scope (verified honestly): the ONLY subprocess timeout in the reconcile orchestration is reconcile.py:454 timeout=60 — and that is on the LOCAL 'rebar list' ticket-fetch, NOT on any Jira/ACLI call. No pass-level watchdog/SIGALRM exists (grep). The REST path (acli_rest _rest_urlopen_with_retry) IS bounded (10-30s/attempt, 3 attempts) — the gap is purely the subprocess path.

Not a same-store race: reconcile serializes same-machine passes via the advisory pass-lock (__main__.py check_pass_lock/acquire_pass_lock + try/finally release). The earlier 30-min hang came from two RAW-primitive probe runs (which bypass the lock), not from a reconcile race — but it surfaced this real unbounded-subprocess gap. Fix per AC: bounded timeout= + TimeoutExpired handling (kill/reap child, retry within _MAX_ATTEMPTS, clear error if exhausted).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
RESEARCH (cited) — two gotchas that will bite the fix; design accordingly:

GOTCHA 1 — process-group reaping. Adding timeout= to subprocess.run is NOT sufficient: run(timeout=) reaps only the DIRECT child; if acli spawns a grandchild (shell/OAuth/network helper) that inherits the capture_output pipe, .communicate() hangs forever and the timeout is a no-op (CPython bpo-30154/31935/37424, closed as dup-not-fixed; AWS CodeGuru 'leaky subprocess timeout' detector). FIX: use Popen(start_new_session=True, stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL, stdout/stderr=PIPE) + communicate(timeout=) and on TimeoutExpired os.killpg(os.getpgid(p.pid), SIGTERM) -> grace -> SIGKILL in finally. stdin=DEVNULL also makes any unexpected acli prompt fail fast instead of blocking (acli prompts by default; pass --yes/--json; this is why --json on link create hung). UNIX/macOS only — Windows would need Job Objects (flag if ever supported).

GOTCHA 2 — retry-after-timeout duplicates writes. A client-side timeout is AMBIGUOUS: the write may have committed server-side. Jira has NO idempotency key; issue create is not idempotent ('2 requests = 2 issues'); POST issueLink does NOT dedupe (Atlassian KB ships SQL to delete duplicate issuelink rows — the REST 'duplicates a link' wording is about the response message, not a uniqueness guarantee). So a blind retry of a CREATE/LINK after timeout creates duplicates. RULE: freely retry READS + connection-refused/5xx (not committed); for WRITES after a timeout, retry ONLY behind a check-before-create/read-back-dedup. NOTE: our outbound _diff_links already dedups links vs existing issuelinks (snapshot) — that is exactly the recommended defense; keep it, and the apply layer should re-check existing links before set_relationship. For issue creates, mark with a stable rebar GUID in a LABEL/field and exact-match query before create (NOT summary free-text; ~5s read-after-write lag can false-negative).

RATE LIMITS (if/when REST): Jira Cloud is cost/points-based; on 429 honor Retry-After (seconds), exponential backoff + jitter, 'only retry if idempotent' (-> ties back to gotcha 2). Best library model: atlassian-python-api urllib3 Retry (status_forcelist 413/429/503, backoff_factor, backoff_jitter, respect_retry_after_header) BUT restrict allowed_methods to idempotent verbs (it wrongly retries POST/PUT/DELETE). pycontribs/jira has NO default timeout = same bug class as this one.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: AC REFINEMENT (from research): the fix MUST use process-group reaping (Popen start_new_session + os.killpg TERM->KILL + stdin=DEVNULL), NOT a bare subprocess.run(timeout=) which is defeated by grandchildren on the pipe. And the timeout-driven RETRY must be write-safe: do not re-issue a create/link after a timeout without a check-before-create/existing-link dedup (Jira is non-idempotent). Bound both per-call and total wall-clock (deadline budget) with exponential backoff + jitter.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
PLAN REVIEW (opus, fitness-for-purpose) — core mechanism is right; risk is concentrated in the write-safety half. VERDICT: not sound to implement exactly as-AC-written; 3 MANDATORY changes first:

C1 (CRITICAL) — write-retry contradiction. _run_acli/_run are GENERIC (cmd: list[str], no read-vs-write knowledge), but AC said 'treat TimeoutExpired as retryable within _MAX_ATTEMPTS' UNCONDITIONALLY. With Jira non-idempotent, that IS the duplicate-write bug. FIX: writes are NON-retried on timeout by default; the read/write decision is made by the CALLER via an explicit flag (e.g. retry_on_timeout/idempotent) — reads (get_issue_links/search/get) pass True; set_relationship/create_issue/delete_issue_link/add_comment pass False. Avoid a brittle in-_run_acli command allowlist. Concrete write-safe deliverable = the already-deferred pre-create get_issue_links existence check at apply_outbound.py:287 + keep _diff_links snapshot dedup + reuse the EXISTING JQL rebar-id:<local> create-dedup in batch_dispatch.create_one (do not reinvent).

C2 (CRITICAL) — retry compounding. Three retry loops exist: _run_acli _MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 (:204), apply_outbound._call_with_retry (batch_dispatch.py:41, retries TimeoutError 3x), batch_dispatch.create_one. subprocess.TimeoutExpired is NOT a builtin TimeoutError. Make the deadline a SINGLE shared budget (not per-loop), and on exhaustion raise a DISTINCT terminal error that _call_with_retry does NOT re-retry — else up to 9x attempts blow the per-pass bound.

I1/N3 (IMPORTANT) — exact kill/reap sequence + the right test. Popen(start_new_session=True, stdin=DEVNULL, PIPE) + communicate(timeout=) + killpg(getpgid(pid),SIGTERM)->grace->SIGKILL is correct, BUT: wrap killpg/getpgid in try/except (ProcessLookupError,PermissionError) for the ESRCH race; AFTER killing call communicate()/wait() again to reap the zombie + drain pipes; capture partial stdout; ensure the timeout path raises BEFORE _check_mutation_failure (never fabricate a success CompletedProcess). TEST with a fake binary that FORKS A GRANDCHILD HOLDING THE PIPE and assert no orphaned process group remains (os.killpg(pgid,0)->ProcessLookupError) — a bare sleep test passes even against a broken bare run(timeout=) and gives false confidence.

Other (lower): stdin=DEVNULL is safe today (acli auth is via its config file, no caller pipes stdin — add a one-line audit note). M1: AC over-claims 'per-pass bounded' — only true given a call-count cap; soften or add a pass deadline. M2: default 120s + env override REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT (acli does OAuth+network, needs more headroom than the 10-30s REST). M3: _advisory_lock/_attestation are GIT subprocesses — only the network git calls (fetch/push, _advisory_lock.py:531-572) need a plain timeout=; don't apply process-group treatment; keep OUT of the _run_acli chokepoint. M4: killpg is UNIX-only — guard with os.name=='posix' or note POSIX-only. N2: route the acli.py:651 delete bypass through the same timeout/reaping. _run_acli IS the right seam.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
=== REVISED PLAN v2 (incorporates the plan review C1/C2/I1 + research gotchas) ===

A. CORE MECHANISM (process-group reap; the load-bearing fix). In _run_acli, replace subprocess.run with:
   Popen(full_cmd, start_new_session=True, stdin=DEVNULL, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, text=True, env=env)
   try: out,err = p.communicate(timeout=call_timeout)
   except TimeoutExpired:
     pgid = os.getpgid(p.pid)   # guard ProcessLookupError
     try: os.killpg(pgid, SIGTERM) except (ProcessLookupError,PermissionError): pass
     try: p.communicate(timeout=GRACE)   # reap + drain after TERM
     except TimeoutExpired: try: os.killpg(pgid, SIGKILL) finally: p.communicate()  # reap zombie
     raise <terminal timeout error>   # BEFORE _check_mutation_failure; never fabricate success
   Capture partial stdout. POSIX-only: guard start_new_session/killpg with os.name=='posix' (note POSIX-only; rebar has no Windows path).

B. TIMEOUTS. Per-call default 120s (acli does OAuth+network; >REST 10-30s) via env REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT. A SINGLE shared per-pass deadline budget threaded in (not per-loop), so retries can't blow wall-clock.

C. RETRY + WRITE-SAFETY (the dangerous half).
   - TimeoutExpired -> a DISTINCT terminal error (e.g. AcliTimeoutError, NOT a builtin TimeoutError) that the outer _call_with_retry (batch_dispatch.py:41) and batch_dispatch loops do NOT re-retry -> avoids 3x*3x compounding.
   - Read vs write decided by the CALLER via explicit flag retry_on_timeout (default False). Reads (get_issue_links/search/get) pass True; writes (set_relationship/create_issue/delete_issue_link/add_comment) pass False -> a timed-out write is NEVER blind-retried.
   - Reuse existing dedup, don't reinvent: batch_dispatch.create_one JQL rebar-id:<local> create-dedup; _diff_links snapshot dedup; ADD the deferred pre-create get_issue_links existence check at apply_outbound.py:287.

D. SCOPE. _run_acli is the chokepoint; route the acli.py:651 delete bypass through the same reaping. Give the NETWORK git subprocesses (fetch/push, _advisory_lock.py:531-572) a plain subprocess.run(timeout=) — NOT the process-group treatment (git rarely spawns pipe-holding grandchildren). Leave the bounded REST path (acli_rest) alone.

E. TESTS (must actually validate gotcha-1). Fake binary that FORKS A GRANDCHILD HOLDING THE PIPE; assert (a) TimeoutExpired within call_timeout+GRACE, (b) NO orphaned process group: capture child pgid, assert os.killpg(pgid,0)->ProcessLookupError after. Plus: write-not-retried-on-timeout; read-retried-on-timeout; partial-stdout captured; _check_mutation_failure not called on killed child.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
=== PRIOR-ART COMPARISON (cited, real GitHub code) — plan CONVERGES strongly; 2 mandatory ADDs ===

Convergence (KEEP, validated against real impls):
- killpg + TERM->grace->KILL->reap matches billiard/celery (billiard/pool.py _trywaitkill: killpg(getpgid(pid),SIGTERM) iff group-leader, 0.1s grace, then KILL+wait) EXACTLY. Most tools (tox 0.2-0.3s, sh, plumbum) kill leader-PID-only — ours is stronger.
- start_new_session=True is the CPython-DOCUMENTED modern choice over preexec_fn=os.setsid (preexec_fn is 'NOT SAFE in presence of threads'). KEEP.
- communicate(timeout=) + post-kill communicate() to reap/drain is the CPython-MANDATED pattern ('proc.kill(); proc.communicate()'); communicate() does NOT deadlock on large output (POSIX uses internal reader threads — salt/plumbum rely on it). KEEP; no manual select loop needed.
- Both ProcessLookupError+PermissionError guards = STRICTER than stdlib send_signal (which only swallows ESRCH). KEEP.
- Retry model maps to prior art: writes-not-retried-by-default = urllib3 DEFAULT_ALLOWED_METHODS excludes POST; conservative default = requests DEFAULT_RETRIES=0; distinct terminal type = tenacity allowlist; caller retry_on_timeout flag = allowed_methods opt-in; pre-create dedup = botocore idempotency tokens. KEEP. (Optional refinement: express as one composable predicate retryable=(read AND transient) OR (write AND retry_on_timeout AND dedup), matching prior-art structure.)

MANDATORY ADDs (gotchas we missed):
1. (a) text=True + kill-mid-stream -> UnicodeDecodeError. CPython communicate() does ONE whole-buffer final .decode(errors='strict') (Lib/subprocess.py _translate_newlines); SIGKILL mid-output truncates a multibyte UTF-8 seq -> the CLEANUP PATH ITSELF CRASHES, masking the timeout error + losing partial stdout. FIX: pass errors='replace' (or capture bytes text=False and decode ourselves). HIGH impact — fires exactly on the kill path we built.
2. (b) D-state / unkillable child: bound the POST-KILL communicate()/wait() with ITS OWN timeout so it can't block forever; treat 'killed but still alive' as a terminal leaked-PID outcome (log), NOT an assert/hang.

OPTIONAL (defense-in-depth, not required for acli): add psutil children(recursive=True) tree-walk as a fallback AFTER the killpg grace pass — catches a grandchild that setsid()s out of our group. killpg-only is ADEQUATE for acli specifically (its hang modes — stdin prompt [killed by DEVNULL], stuck socket, JVM/helper grandchild holding the pipe — all stay in our session; acli won't daemonize out).

VERDICT: strategy converges strongly with mature prior art. Implement plan v2 + the two ADDs (errors='replace'; bounded post-kill drain). Plan is then ready.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
=== VALIDATION SPIKES (ran code to prove the moving parts BEFORE implementing) ===
All decisive, reproducible. Fake 'acli' = a binary that forks a grandchild which inherits the stdout PIPE and hangs (the exact gotcha-1 shape). Results:

E1 (failure mode): bare subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, timeout=2) -> TimeoutExpired raised, but the GRANDCHILD is left ORPHANED (alive=True). Confirms run() only reaps the direct child -> proves WHY we need the process-group fix.
E2 (the fix): Popen(start_new_session=True, stdin=DEVNULL, stdout/stderr=PIPE) + communicate(timeout=2) -> TimeoutExpired; then os.killpg(os.getpgid(pid), SIGKILL) -> grandchild alive=False. Group kill reaps the whole session incl. the pipe-holding grandchild. FIX VALIDATED.
E3 (text decode gotcha): child emits a truncated multibyte UTF-8 lead (b'ok-\xe2\x82') then hangs. With text=True errors='strict' -> communicate() raises UnicodeDecodeError ('unexpected end of data') ON THE CLEANUP PATH (masks the timeout, loses partial stdout). With errors='replace' -> decodes OK, no crash. CONFIRMS the gotcha AND the fix.
E4 (retry type): issubclass(subprocess.TimeoutExpired, builtin TimeoutError) == False. So _call_with_retry (batch_dispatch.py:73 'except TimeoutError') will NOT catch/re-retry our timeout — PROVIDED our terminal error is NOT a builtin-TimeoutError subclass. Validates the 'distinct terminal error' design (and shows a naive raise TimeoutError would be silently re-retried = the duplicate-write bug).
E5 (ESRCH race): killpg on an already-exited group -> ProcessLookupError. Confirms the try/except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError) guard is REQUIRED.

Pitfalls caught pre-implementation: (1) must use errors='replace' (E3) or the kill path crashes; (2) terminal error must not subclass builtin TimeoutError (E4) or _call_with_retry re-retries writes; (3) killpg needs ESRCH guard (E5). The core group-reap mechanism works (E1->E2). Cleanup hygiene note for the eventual UNIT test: the experiment's own orphaned-PID check raced (grandchild died just after the assert) — the test must poll-with-timeout for 'group gone' (os.killpg(pgid,0)->ProcessLookupError), not assert instantaneously.

PLAN STATUS: approach validated end-to-end; v2 + the 2 ADDs (errors='replace'; bounded post-kill drain) is ready to implement.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
=== LINK TESTING IMPROVEMENTS ROLLED INTO THIS TICKET (SDET review C1/C2/I1 + link minor) ===
These belong here: C2's fix IS this ticket's write-safety deliverable (the pre-create get_issue_links existence check), and C1/I1 test the link dedup/apply path that the write-safe retry touches.

C1 — link dedup/echo-suppression has ZERO deterministic CI coverage (currently only exercised in gated external tests). ADD CI-tier unit tests in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/:
  (a) local 'blocks' dep whose Jira issuelinks already carries (Blocks, target_key) -> outbound_differ._diff_links returns [] (no re-ADD);
  (b) inbound issuelink already present in local deps -> inbound_differ._diff_links_inbound returns [];
  (c) add a 6th case to tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py (it hardcodes deps:[] at L117, omitting the link relationship type's echo test).
  Also REWRITE the stale/false docstring in tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_link_sync.py L20-24 (it still claims links are hardcoded [] and InboundMutation has no links field — both now exist).

C2 — duplicate-link-on-retry: ADD a unit test with set_relationship side_effect=[URLError('timeout'), None] asserting AT-MOST-ONCE server effect (call_count==1 via the new pre-create get_issue_links check). Land it as xfail(strict=True) FIRST (tracks the hole), then the write-safe fix (caller retry_on_timeout=False for set_relationship + pre-create existence check at apply_outbound.py:287) flips it to passing. Also add set_relationship.call_count==1 to the happy-path test (links_applied counter alone can't distinguish 1 link from 2).

I1 — inbound apply over-mocks rebar.link (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_inbound_links.py:54-106 stubs the entire job, asserts only arg pass-through). ADD a real-tmp_path-store integration test asserting the link is observable via ticket_deps, plus an invalid-relation rejection test (links_applied==0).

Link minor — test_link_sync.py:140 accepts to_key/target/to triple-fallback; tighten to the settled 'to_key' now that the contract is fixed.

These ride with the d843 implementation as its test+write-safety coverage. Non-link SDET findings (C3/C4/I2-I7/minors) are separate tickets discovered_from 6623.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
=== PLAN v3 (resolves the approval-gate CHANGES-REQUIRED: C1, I1, I2 + M1/M3/N1) ===

C1 RESOLVED — FULL caller enumeration (read => retry_on_timeout=True; write => False default). _run_acli AND AcliClient._run both take retry_on_timeout; _run forwards it (acli.py:250). All sites:
ROUTE A (self._run): acli.py:387 search_issues READ=True; acli.py:590 get_comments READ=True; acli_graph.py:77 get_issue_link_types READ=True; acli_graph.py:142 add_label WRITE=False; :185 remove_label WRITE=False; :433 update_comment WRITE=False; :473 set_relationship WRITE=False; :513 delete_issue_link WRITE=False.
ROUTE B (direct _run_acli): acli.py:205 update_issue WRITE=False; acli_cli_ops.py:250 create WRITE=False; :291 create_from_json WRITE=False; :454 get_issue READ=True; :484 add_comment WRITE=False; :524 get_comments READ=True.
=> 5 READS must be explicitly True (search_issues/get_comments x2/get_issue_link_types/get_issue) — defaulting a read to False is a SILENT regression (loses timeout-retry for the dedup JQL search + get_issue). 9 WRITES default False (safe). A test must assert each read passes True and each write False (e.g. introspect/parametrize) so a new caller can't silently mis-default.

I2 RESOLVED — NO shared per-pass deadline threading (over-engineering; infeasible without a cross-cutting refactor). Worst-case is bounded deterministically by: per-call timeout (120s) x (bounded _MAX_ATTEMPTS retries, reads only) + the EXISTING call-count ceiling (batch_dispatch.py:125 rest_calls>=200 budget caps creates). Worst-case per call = call_timeout + GRACE + drain. Soften AC from 'per-pass bounded' to 'per-call bounded; per-pass bounded given existing call-count caps'.

I1 RESOLVED — scope D corrected. _advisory_lock.py:531-572 are LOCAL ops (worktree prune/remove, diff, rev-parse) — NO fetch/push there (grep: zero). The real network git (git fetch _store/sync.py:116; git push _store/push.py; git verify-commit _attestation.py:107) is OUTSIDE the acli path and is DEFERRED to its own ticket (c16f-54f5-cd11-4e51). d843 scope = the acli subprocess chokepoint ONLY (no git timeouts in d843).

M1 — GRACE = 3s (acli JVM/helper children need longer than billiard 0.1s to flush on SIGTERM); post-kill drain bound = 2s. Worst-case per call = call_timeout(120) + 3 + 2.
M3 — partial stdout/stderr come from the ORIGINAL TimeoutExpired.stdout/.stderr; the post-kill communicate()/wait() return ('',''), so capture from the original exception, not the reap calls.
M2 — errors='replace' goes on Popen (with text=True); the delete bypass (acli.py:643-652) must route through the chokepoint and inherit it (drop its bare subprocess.run).
N1 — DOCUMENTED RESIDUAL RISK: create_issue timeout after server-commit -> AcliTimeoutError (un-retried, correct), but the rebar-id label is written AFTER create (batch_dispatch.py:193), so the next pass's dedup JQL false-negatives -> duplicate issue. The pre-create existence check is for LINKS, not creates. Acknowledged residual (matches apply_outbound.py:282-289 comment style); not fixed in d843.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: PLAN v3 APPROVED by opus re-review (all blockers C1/I2/I1 + minors resolved, verified against code; verdict APPROVE, ready to implement). Implementing in two passes: (1) core acli-subprocess hardening + retry_on_timeout plumbing + unit tests; (2) link write-safety (pre-create get_issue_links check) + rolled-in link tests (C1/C2/I1/minor).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
PASS 1 IMPLEMENTED (core acli-subprocess hardening + retry_on_timeout plumbing + unit tests). NOT committed; Pass-2 link write-safety NOT done.

acli_subprocess.py: added AcliTimeoutError (NOT a builtin TimeoutError subclass — spike E4) carrying cmd + partial stdout/stderr; rewrote _run_acli to Popen(start_new_session POSIX-only, stdin=DEVNULL, PIPE, text, errors='replace') + communicate(timeout=call_timeout); on TimeoutExpired -> _decode_partial(exc.stdout/.stderr) (M3, bytes-safe), _reap_process_group (killpg TERM->grace(3s)->KILL->bounded drain(2s), ESRCH/EPERM-guarded, D-state-safe leaked-PID log; non-posix p.kill()+wait fallback). retry_on_timeout=True reads retry within the existing loop; writes raise terminal AcliTimeoutError BEFORE _check_mutation_failure. Preserved CalledProcessError retry/backoff + auth(401)/assignee fast-aborts. Env REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT (default 120, bad value->120); GRACE=3, DRAIN=2 constants.

Plumbing: AcliClient._run + _run_acli take retry_on_timeout (default False). READS explicit True: search_issues(acli.py:387), get_comments(acli.py:590), get_issue_link_types(acli_graph.py:77), get_issue(acli_cli_ops:454), get_comments(acli_cli_ops:524). WRITES default False: update_issue(acli.py:205), add_label/remove_label/update_comment/set_relationship/delete_issue_link(acli_graph), create/create_from_json/add_comment(acli_cli_ops). Delete bypass (acli.py ~643) routed through _run_acli (write, retry_on_timeout=False); preserves 404-idempotent/403-permission semantics.

Tests: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py (10 tests, POSIX-guarded): grandchild process-group reap (poll for group gone), write-not-retried(1 spawn), read-retried(_MAX_ATTEMPTS spawns), AcliTimeoutError not builtin TimeoutError, truncated-UTF8 no crash on reap path, partial stdout captured, _check_mutation_failure not called on killed child, classification guard (5 reads=True/6 writes=False via _run_acli introspection), delete routes through chokepoint. Fixed 3 pre-existing stubs to accept retry_on_timeout (conftest fake_run_acli, cross_mixin fake_run, bridge delete test repointed subprocess.run->_run_acli).

Regression: reconciler+integration+scripts 1216 passed/1 skipped; broad 'not integration and not external' 1780 passed/7 skipped. ruff check + format clean. No orphaned processes. No deviations from PLAN v3.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
DONE — implemented per the approved+code-validated plan v3, in two passes, both opus-reviewed + independently verified.
PASS 1 (e44e36c2): bounded every acli call via the _run_acli chokepoint — Popen(start_new_session POSIX, stdin=DEVNULL, text=True, errors='replace') + communicate(timeout=REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT default 120, <=0 falls back) + process-group reap (getpgid ESRCH/EPERM-guarded -> killpg SIGTERM -> 3s grace -> SIGKILL -> 2s bounded drain, D-state safe). Terminal AcliTimeoutError (NOT a builtin TimeoutError) raised before _check_mutation_failure. retry_on_timeout flag: 5 reads retry, 9 writes terminal (a non-idempotent write timeout is never blind-retried; AcliTimeoutError isn't caught by _call_with_retry so no compounding). Delete bypass routed through the chokepoint. 10 unit tests incl. a real grandchild-holding-pipe process-group reap + read/write classification guard; mutation-tested (subclass-TimeoutError and orphan-group both caught).
PASS 2 (93219076): link write-safety — live pre-create get_issue_links existence check (cached once/update, direction-agnostic, non-fatal) closes the cross-pass/agent + timeout duplicate windows; intra-_run_acli CalledProcessError-retry window documented as narrow pre-existing residual.
